flashcards.front: What is the basic principle of Selection Sort?
flashcards.back: Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted portion and swapping it with the first unsorted element.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
flashcards.back: O(n²) for all cases (best, average, and worst) because it always performs n(n-1)/2 comparisons.

flashcards.front: How many swaps does Selection Sort perform?
flashcards.back: Exactly n-1 swaps in all cases, where n is the number of elements in the array.
No, Selection Sort is not stable by default because it may change the relative order of equal elements during swaps.

flashcards.front: When is Selection Sort particularly useful?
flashcards.back: Selection Sort is useful when memory writes are expensive because it minimizes the number of swaps (only n-1 swaps).
